title: tournefortia restrepoae
intro: Tournefortia restrepoae, also known as Restrepo's tournefortia, is a species of flowering plant in the bignonia family, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers and a woody stem. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its extracts. The health benefits of Tournefortia restrepoae include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, as well as its use in treating various gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its therapeutic properties.
